subject: general-knowledge
description: Maps & Locations MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: Which seaport is known as the 'Coffee Port' of the world?
mcqs.explanation: Santos in Brazil is known as the 'Coffee Port' of the world — Latin America's largest port and a major coffee export hub.

mcqs.q: Which is the busiest port of the United States, located on San Pedro Bay?
mcqs.explanation: The Port of Los Angeles on San Pedro Bay is the busiest container port in the USA.

mcqs.q: Ashkelon was the oldest seaport of the world located in:
mcqs.explanation: Ashkelon — an ancient Mediterranean port in Ancient Palestine — is among the oldest seaports in the world.

mcqs.q: Szczecin seaport, on the Baltic Sea, is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Szczecin is a major Polish port on the Baltic Sea.

mcqs.q: Which of the following statements is correct?
options: Abadan seaport is present in Iran
Port of Savannah is the 'Gateway to the South' (Georgia)
The most leading port of India is Mumbai
mcqs.explanation: All three statements are correct: Abadan (Iran), Savannah (Georgia, USA — 'Gateway to the South'), and Mumbai (India).

mcqs.q: Tanjung Priok Seaport is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Tanjung Priok in Jakarta is the main port of Indonesia.

mcqs.q: Chennai Port, formerly known as ____, is the second-largest port of India after Mumbai.
mcqs.explanation: Chennai Port was formerly called Madras Port — India's second-largest port after Mumbai.

mcqs.q: Port of Shanghai is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Shanghai is China's largest port and the world's busiest container port.

mcqs.q: Which of the following statements is correct?
options: Alexandria is the seaport of Egypt
Dickson is the seaport of Malaysia
Seattle is the seaport of USA
mcqs.explanation: Alexandria (Egypt), Port Dickson (Malaysia) and Seattle (USA) are all correct.

mcqs.q: Puerto La Guaira seaport is of:
mcqs.explanation: La Guaira is the principal seaport of Venezuela.

mcqs.q: Sabang seaport is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Sabang is a seaport in Aceh province of Indonesia.

mcqs.q: Karachi, Bin Qasim, Pasni, Jiwani, Gadani, Ormara and Gwadar are the seaports of:
mcqs.explanation: All seven are seaports of Pakistan along the Arabian Sea coast.

mcqs.q: Which is the largest seaport of Pakistan?
mcqs.explanation: Karachi is the largest seaport of Pakistan, founded in 1854 by the British.

mcqs.q: Which of the following statements is correct?
options: Salalah is a seaport of Oman
Port Said is a seaport of Egypt
Rotterdam is the largest port of Netherlands
mcqs.explanation: All three are correct: Salalah (Oman), Port Said (Egypt) and Rotterdam (largest port of Netherlands).

mcqs.q: Haifa is the seaport of:
mcqs.explanation: Haifa is Israel's largest seaport, on the Mediterranean coast.

mcqs.q: In which country is the Alexandria seaport located?
mcqs.explanation: Alexandria is Egypt's main Mediterranean seaport, founded by Alexander the Great in 331 BCE.

mcqs.q: In which country is the Amsterdam seaport located?
mcqs.explanation: Amsterdam is a major Dutch seaport, though Rotterdam is the largest in the Netherlands.

mcqs.q: In which country is the Antwerp seaport located?
mcqs.explanation: Antwerp is Belgium's main port and one of Europe's busiest.

mcqs.q: Which of the following statements is correct?
options: Aden is the seaport of Yemen
Dammam is the seaport of Saudi Arabia
mcqs.explanation: All three are correct: Chittagong (Bangladesh), Aden (Yemen) and Dammam (Saudi Arabia).

mcqs.q: Naples is the seaport of:
mcqs.explanation: Naples is one of Italy's most important Mediterranean seaports.

mcqs.q: In which country is the Cardiff seaport located?
mcqs.explanation: Cardiff is the capital of Wales (UK) and a historic British seaport.

mcqs.q: In which country is the Oslo seaport located?
mcqs.explanation: Oslo is the capital and main seaport of Norway.

mcqs.q: In which country is the Penang seaport located?
mcqs.explanation: Penang is a Malaysian state and major seaport on the Strait of Malacca.

mcqs.q: In which country is the Le Havre seaport located?
mcqs.explanation: Le Havre is a major French seaport on the English Channel, at the mouth of the Seine.

mcqs.q: Where is the seaport of Aalborg located?
mcqs.explanation: Aalborg is a Danish seaport in Jutland.

mcqs.q: Where is the seaport of Almeria located?
mcqs.explanation: Almeria is a Mediterranean seaport in southern Spain.

mcqs.q: Naples is the seaport of:
mcqs.explanation: Naples is one of Italy's largest Mediterranean seaports (south Italy).

mcqs.q: Which of the following statements is correct?
options: Port of San Antonio is the seaport of Chile
Açu is the seaport of Brazil
Port of Tianjin is the seaport of China
mcqs.explanation: All correct: San Antonio (Chile), Açu (Brazil) and Tianjin (China).

mcqs.q: Ho Chi Minh seaport was formerly known as:
mcqs.explanation: Ho Chi Minh City was formerly Saigon — its seaport is now Vietnam's largest.

mcqs.q: Which is the main port of Malaysia?
mcqs.explanation: Port Klang is Malaysia's largest and busiest seaport.

mcqs.q: 'Seattle' is the seaport of which country?
mcqs.explanation: Seattle is a major US seaport on Puget Sound, Washington state.

mcqs.q: 'Darwin' is the seaport of:
mcqs.explanation: Darwin is the capital of the Northern Territory and an Australian seaport.

mcqs.q: 'Montreal' is the seaport of:
mcqs.explanation: Montreal is a major Canadian inland seaport on the St Lawrence River.

mcqs.q: Alexandria is the seaport of:
mcqs.explanation: Alexandria is Egypt's main Mediterranean seaport.

mcqs.q: 'Rio de Janeiro' is the seaport of:
mcqs.explanation: Rio de Janeiro is a major Brazilian seaport on the Atlantic.

mcqs.q: 'Buenos Aires' is the seaport of:
mcqs.explanation: Buenos Aires, capital of Argentina, is one of South America's largest seaports.

mcqs.q: 'Bandar Bushehr' is the seaport of:
mcqs.explanation: Bandar Bushehr is a major Iranian seaport on the Persian Gulf.

mcqs.q: 'Lisbon' is the seaport of which country?
mcqs.explanation: Lisbon, capital of Portugal, is its main Atlantic seaport.

mcqs.q: 'Liverpool' is the seaport of:
mcqs.explanation: Liverpool is a historic British seaport on the Mersey, NW England.

mcqs.q: Which of the following statements is correct?
options: Port of Vancouver is located in Canada
Latakia is the seaport of Syria
Honolulu is the seaport of USA
mcqs.explanation: All correct: Vancouver (Canada), Latakia (Syria) and Honolulu (USA / Hawaii).

mcqs.q: Who were the first people to climb Mount Everest in 1953?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Edmund Hillary (New Zealand) and Tenzing Norgay (Nepal) summited Everest on 29 May 1953.

mcqs.q: Junko Tabei was the first woman to summit Mount Everest. What was her nationality?
mcqs.explanation: Junko Tabei of Japan was the first woman to summit Everest (1975).

mcqs.q: Magellan is to the Philippines as Cook is to where?
mcqs.explanation: Captain James Cook explored and died in Hawaii (1779), just as Magellan died in the Philippines (1521).

mcqs.q: Erik the Red is to Greenland as Leif Eriksson is to where?
mcqs.explanation: Leif Eriksson, son of Erik the Red, reached and named Vinland (North America) ~1000 CE.

mcqs.q: Which explorer named the Pacific Ocean?
mcqs.explanation: Ferdinand Magellan named the Pacific Ocean during his 1519-22 circumnavigation.

mcqs.q: Who is credited with the first visit to the North Pole?
mcqs.explanation: Robert E. Peary is credited with reaching the North Pole in 1909.

mcqs.q: Who unknowingly discovered the Western Hemisphere?
mcqs.explanation: Christopher Columbus reached the Americas in 1492, thinking he had reached Asia.

mcqs.q: Who was the first to call America a 'New World'?
mcqs.explanation: Italian explorer Amerigo Vespucci first called the Americas the 'New World'. The continent is named after him.

mcqs.q: Which Yale professor brought Machu Picchu to international attention?
mcqs.explanation: American historian Hiram Bingham (Yale) rediscovered Machu Picchu in 1911.

mcqs.q: Which explorer pushed Chinese maritime exploration and trade to its farthest extent?
mcqs.explanation: Ming-dynasty admiral Cheng Ho (Zheng He) led 7 great voyages 1405-1433, reaching Africa.

mcqs.q: Sweden's most prolific explorer who crossed the deserts and mountains of Central Asia was:
mcqs.explanation: Sven Anders Hedin was Sweden's most prolific explorer of Central Asia.

mcqs.q: In 1325, which Islamic scholar from Tangier set out for Mecca and began a journey of three decades and 75,000 miles?
mcqs.explanation: Ibn Battuta of Tangier began his epic journeys in 1325, covering ~75,000 miles across Eurasia and Africa.

mcqs.q: Who established a short-lived settlement at a site called Vinland?
mcqs.explanation: Leif Eriksson established a short-lived Vinland (Newfoundland) settlement around 1000 CE.

mcqs.q: The biggest achievement of Roald Amundsen's career was to be the first man to reach which place?
mcqs.explanation: Roald Amundsen and his team reached the South Pole on 14 December 1911 — a first.

mcqs.q: Which Spaniard was the first to cross the Isthmus of Panama and reach the Pacific Ocean?
mcqs.explanation: In 1513, Vasco Núñez de Balboa crossed the Isthmus of Panama and became the first European to see the Pacific from the Americas.

mcqs.q: Which European discoverer of the Mississippi River had to be buried in secret when he died?
mcqs.explanation: Hernando de Soto, Spanish explorer of the Mississippi (1541), was buried secretly in the river by his men in 1542.

mcqs.q: Which Norwegian explorer was the first to explore both Poles and died during a rescue mission?
mcqs.explanation: Roald Amundsen reached both Poles and died on 18 June 1928 during a rescue flight for Umberto Nobile.

mcqs.q: Which explorer first circumnavigated the globe?
mcqs.explanation: Ferdinand Magellan led the first circumnavigation (1519-22), though he died in the Philippines. His crew completed the voyage.

mcqs.q: Which Scottish missionary and explorer was the first European to discover Victoria Falls?
mcqs.explanation: Scottish missionary David Livingstone first sighted Victoria Falls in 1855, naming them after Queen Victoria.

mcqs.q: Which Spanish explorer conquered the Incas in Peru and founded Lima in 1535?
mcqs.explanation: Francisco Pizarro conquered the Inca Empire and founded Lima in 1535.

mcqs.q: How old was Ibn Battuta when he began his travels?
mcqs.explanation: Ibn Battuta began his travels at age 21 in 1325.

mcqs.q: Of what religion was Ibn Battuta?
mcqs.explanation: Ibn Battuta was a Muslim scholar from Tangier.

mcqs.q: Which country was Ibn Battuta born in?
mcqs.explanation: Ibn Battuta was born in Tangier, Morocco in 1304.

mcqs.q: Which Italian explored the east coast of North America for the French in 1524?
mcqs.explanation: Italian explorer Giovanni da Verrazzano explored the east coast of North America for France in 1524.

mcqs.q: Which explorer made voyages for the English and the Dutch and explored what is now New York?
mcqs.explanation: Henry Hudson sailed for the English and Dutch and explored the river now bearing his name (New York).

mcqs.q: What was the other name for the Lewis and Clark Expedition?
options: The Scientific Exploration of the Western United States
The Exploration for Scientific Purposes to the Pacific
mcqs.explanation: Officially called the 'Corps of Discovery' (1804-06), it explored the Louisiana Purchase territory.

mcqs.q: In 1492, which Italian mariner and navigator landed in the West Indies?
mcqs.explanation: Christopher Columbus landed in the West Indies on 12 October 1492.

mcqs.q: In 1498, which Portuguese explorer completed the first voyage from Portugal to India?
mcqs.explanation: Vasco da Gama completed the first sea voyage Portugal → India in 1498.

mcqs.q: Columbus set sail in 1492 with the Nina, the Pinta and the Santa Maria. On which date did he set sail?
mcqs.explanation: Columbus departed Palos de la Frontera, Spain on 2 August 1492 (he made landfall in the Bahamas on 12 October).

mcqs.q: Who discovered the sea route from Europe to India?
mcqs.explanation: Vasco da Gama discovered the sea route Europe → India in 1498.

mcqs.q: Who discovered Greenland?
mcqs.explanation: Norseman Erik the Red discovered and named Greenland ~982 CE.

mcqs.q: Who was the first person to reach the South Pole?
mcqs.explanation: Roald Amundsen reached the South Pole on 14 December 1911.

mcqs.q: Roald Amundsen reached the South Pole in:
mcqs.explanation: Amundsen reached the South Pole on 14 December 1911.

mcqs.q: Who was the first person to reach the North Pole?
mcqs.explanation: Robert E. Peary is credited with reaching the North Pole in 1909.

mcqs.q: Robert Peary reached the North Pole in:
mcqs.explanation: Robert Peary reached the North Pole on 6 April 1909.

mcqs.q: Who discovered Australia?
mcqs.explanation: Dutch navigator Willem Janszoon was the first European to land on Australia (1606).

mcqs.q: Who journeyed through China, India and other parts of Asia from 1271-94?
mcqs.explanation: Venetian merchant Marco Polo travelled across Asia 1271-1295, including 17 years at the court of Kublai Khan.

mcqs.q: America was discovered by:
mcqs.explanation: Christopher Columbus is credited with the European discovery of America in 1492.

mcqs.q: Who discovered the sea route from Europe to India?
mcqs.explanation: Vasco da Gama discovered the sea route Europe → India in 1498.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the CN Tower?
mcqs.explanation: The CN Tower (553 m) is in Toronto, Canada — completed 1976.

mcqs.q: Yangon (formerly Rangoon) was the capital of Myanmar — given the name in 1755. What is the meaning of 'Yangon'?
mcqs.explanation: Yangon means 'End of strife / End of war' in Burmese.

mcqs.q: Kiev (capital of Ukraine) is given which name by Russians?
mcqs.explanation: Russians historically call Kiev 'Mother of cities' (Mother of Russian cities).

mcqs.q: Which was the most important city of the world in 7000 BC?
mcqs.explanation: Jericho is among the oldest continuously inhabited cities, dating to ~9000 BC.

mcqs.q: Where can you find Troy?
mcqs.explanation: The ruins of ancient Troy are in northwestern Turkey (Çanakkale province).

mcqs.q: Phoenix Park is located in which country?
mcqs.explanation: Phoenix Park is in Dublin, Ireland — one of Europe's largest urban parks.

mcqs.q: The 900-year-old Altit Fort received the 2011 UNESCO Asia-Pacific Award for Cultural Heritage Conservation. Where is it located?
mcqs.explanation: Altit Fort is in Hunza Valley, Gilgit-Baltistan, Pakistan — ~900 years old.

mcqs.q: Abu Salim prison is a top-security prison in:
mcqs.explanation: Abu Salim is a high-security prison in Tripoli, Libya — notorious for the 1996 massacre.

mcqs.q: Alexander Gardens — one of the first urban public parks — is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Alexander Gardens are a historic public garden adjacent to the Kremlin in Moscow.

mcqs.q: Hyde Park — famous for its Speakers' Corner — is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Hyde Park is in Central London with the famous Speakers' Corner at Marble Arch.

mcqs.q: George Square — the principal civic square — is located in:
mcqs.explanation: George Square is the main civic square of Glasgow, Scotland.

mcqs.q: St Andrew Square is located in:
mcqs.explanation: St Andrew Square is a Georgian square in Edinburgh's New Town.

mcqs.q: Tahrir Square is a major public square located in:
mcqs.explanation: Tahrir Square ('Liberation Square') is in central Cairo — the epicentre of the 2011 Arab Spring in Egypt.

mcqs.q: Martyrs' Square (also Green Square) is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Martyrs' Square (renamed from Green Square in 2011) is in Tripoli, Libya.

mcqs.q: Red Square is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Red Square is the iconic central square of Moscow, beside the Kremlin.

mcqs.q: Tiananmen Square — a large city square — is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Tiananmen Square in central Beijing is among the world's largest public squares.

mcqs.q: Lobnoye Mesto, also known as the 'Place of Skulls', is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Lobnoye Mesto is a stone platform in Red Square, Moscow.

mcqs.q: Trafalgar Square is a public space and tourist attraction in:
mcqs.explanation: Trafalgar Square in central London commemorates the 1805 Battle of Trafalgar.

mcqs.q: Belfast (Ireland) is known for which industry?
mcqs.explanation: Belfast was historically famous for shipbuilding — Harland & Wolff built the Titanic there.

mcqs.q: Béthune, a city of France, is known for which industry?
mcqs.explanation: Béthune in Pas-de-Calais was a major coalfield town.

mcqs.q: Buenos Aires (Argentina) is famous for which industry?
mcqs.explanation: Buenos Aires and the Pampas region are famous for dairy and beef.

mcqs.q: The 'cork' industry is associated with Cadiz, a city of:
mcqs.explanation: Cadiz, in Andalusia, Spain, is a centre of the cork industry.

mcqs.q: Bangladesh is known for the production of:
mcqs.explanation: Bangladesh is the world's leading producer of jute — its 'golden fibre'.

mcqs.q: Lumbini, the birthplace of Siddhartha Gautama (Buddha), is located in which district?
mcqs.explanation: Lumbini, birthplace of Lord Buddha, is in Rupandehi/Kapilavastu region of Nepal.

mcqs.q: Which US city is famous for agricultural implements, grain and meat market?
mcqs.explanation: Chicago has historically been the centre of US grain markets and the meat-packing industry.

mcqs.q: Which Pakistani city has the highest annual rainfall?
mcqs.explanation: Among the options, Chitral typically has higher annual rainfall than the others.

mcqs.q: Sudbury (Canada) is the source of:
mcqs.explanation: Sudbury in Ontario, Canada, is a major source of silver (and nickel/copper).

mcqs.q: 'Silicon Valley' is famous for:
mcqs.explanation: Silicon Valley in California is the global hub of the computer industry.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Golden Pavilion?
mcqs.explanation: The Golden Pavilion (Kinkaku-ji) is a Zen Buddhist temple in Kyoto, Japan.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Great Buddha?
mcqs.explanation: The Great Buddha of Kamakura is a famous bronze statue in Japan.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Great Minaret of Samarra Mosque?
mcqs.explanation: The Malwiya / Great Minaret of Samarra (9th century CE) is in Iraq.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Great Mosque of Timbuktu?
mcqs.explanation: The Great Mosque of Timbuktu (Djinguereber Mosque) is in Mali.

mcqs.q: Where can you find Kościuszko Mound?
mcqs.explanation: Kościuszko Mound is in Kraków, Poland — honouring Tadeusz Kościuszko.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Louvre Pyramide?
mcqs.explanation: The glass Louvre Pyramide is in the courtyard of the Louvre Museum in Paris.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Mayan / Aztec Temple?
mcqs.explanation: The Maya and Aztec temple ruins are concentrated in Mexico and Central America.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Edinburgh National Monument?
mcqs.explanation: The Edinburgh National Monument on Calton Hill is in Edinburgh, Scotland.

mcqs.q: Where can you find Notre Dame Cathedral?
mcqs.explanation: Notre-Dame de Paris is the iconic Gothic cathedral on Île de la Cité, Paris.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Opera House?
mcqs.explanation: The Sydney Opera House is the iconic landmark of Sydney, Australia.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Potala Palace?
mcqs.explanation: Potala Palace, the historic residence of the Dalai Lamas, is in Lhasa, Tibet.

mcqs.q: Where can you find Christ the Redeemer?
mcqs.explanation: Christ the Redeemer statue overlooks Rio de Janeiro from Mount Corcovado.

mcqs.q: Where can you find St. Peter's Basilica?
mcqs.explanation: St. Peter's Basilica is in Vatican City, within Rome, Italy.

mcqs.q: USA's Detroit city is associated with which industry?
mcqs.explanation: Detroit is the historic capital of the US automobile industry — 'Motor City'.

mcqs.q: The Leaning Tower of Pisa is in:
mcqs.explanation: The Leaning Tower of Pisa stands in the Piazza dei Miracoli, Pisa, Italy.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Sphinx?
mcqs.explanation: The Great Sphinx of Giza guards the Pyramids at Giza, Egypt.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Alamo Mission?
mcqs.explanation: The Alamo Mission is in San Antonio, Texas — site of the famous 1836 battle.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Azadi Monument?
mcqs.explanation: The Azadi Tower (Freedom Tower) is the gateway monument of Tehran, Iran.

mcqs.q: In London, no visit is complete without the famous gardens with the elegant Palm House. They are:
mcqs.explanation: The Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew (Kew Gardens) in London house the iconic Victorian Palm House.

mcqs.q: Where can you find Castel Sant'Angelo?
mcqs.explanation: Castel Sant'Angelo (Hadrian's Mausoleum) is in Rome, near Vatican City.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Castle of La Mota?
mcqs.explanation: The Castle of La Mota is in Medina del Campo, Spain.

mcqs.q: Which palace has gardens including formal knot gardens, the Broad Walk and an intricate maze?
mcqs.explanation: Buckingham Palace's gardens include the famous knot gardens, the Broad Walk and a hedge maze.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Giza Pyramids?
mcqs.explanation: The Giza Pyramids are on the Giza plateau near Cairo, Egypt.

mcqs.q: Where can you find Diamond Head?
mcqs.explanation: Diamond Head is a famous volcanic tuff cone on Oahu, Hawaii.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Dome of the Rock?
mcqs.explanation: The Dome of the Rock stands on the Temple Mount in Jerusalem.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Grand Canyon?
mcqs.explanation: The Grand Canyon (carved by the Colorado River) is in Arizona, USA — often referred via its Colorado-River source.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Colosseum?
mcqs.explanation: The Roman Colosseum (Flavian Amphitheatre) is in Rome, Italy.

mcqs.q: Where can you find Old Faithful, Yellowstone National Park?
mcqs.explanation: Old Faithful geyser is in Yellowstone National Park, USA (mostly Wyoming).

mcqs.q: Where can you find Red Square?
mcqs.explanation: Red Square is the iconic central square of Moscow, Russia.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Blue Mosque?
mcqs.explanation: The Blue Mosque (Sultan Ahmed Mosque) is in Istanbul, Turkey.

mcqs.q: Where can you find Mount Eden crater?
mcqs.explanation: Mount Eden is a volcanic crater in Auckland, New Zealand.

mcqs.q: Gujranwala (Pakistan) is known for:
mcqs.explanation: Gujranwala is industrially known for iron, safes, almrahs, rice and utensils.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Blue-domed Church?
mcqs.explanation: The iconic blue-domed churches are on Santorini, Greece (Oia village).

mcqs.q: Where can you find St. Basil's Cathedral?
mcqs.explanation: St. Basil's Cathedral, with its colourful onion domes, stands in Red Square, Moscow.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Great Sphinx?
mcqs.explanation: The Great Sphinx of Giza stands on the Giza Plateau, Egypt.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the Little Mermaid statue?
mcqs.explanation: The Little Mermaid bronze statue is on the waterfront in Copenhagen, Denmark.

mcqs.q: Where can you find the famous Windmills?
mcqs.explanation: The 19 windmills of Kinderdijk in the Netherlands are a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

mcqs.q: Where can you find Hagia Sophia (Agia Sofia)?
mcqs.explanation: Hagia Sophia is the iconic Byzantine-era basilica/mosque in Istanbul, Turkey.

mcqs.q: Which city is associated with tobacco and cigars?
mcqs.explanation: Havana, Cuba, is famous worldwide for cigars (e.g. Cohiba, Montecristo).
